attacking_style: Employs disruptive, high-risk attacks in the final 1-1.5km of sprint stages, specifically targeting the moment when rival leadout trains are most vulnerable and committed to their structure.
team_tactics: Functions as both leadout man and tactical disruptor for Bora-Hansgrohe. Positions teammates through final 2km with precision timing, avoiding premature positioning errors that catch rivals.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint finishes where leadout train disruption creates maximum chaos. Thrives in tactical finale situations requiring late positioning rather than pure power.
vulnerability: Suicide attacks are tactically effective but self-sacrificing—destroys rival structures but eliminates own result potential. Benefits teammates and opportunists more than himself.
key_patterns: Launches late-race attacks (1100m) specifically timed to collapse organized leadout trains (2 observations)
Dual role: precise leadout positioning for teammates and tactical kamikaze disruption (2 observations)
Creates chaos that benefits freelance sprinters rather than structured teams (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Consistent tactical intelligence across 2018-2019, executing team role effectively but personal results secondary to team disruption strategy
